description_long: This is a dark green, twin-engine military airplane with a streamlined fuselage and broad, straight wings. The wings are low-mounted and feature white cross insignias, indicating a historical or military context, likely from the World War II era. The engines are mounted on the wings, each with a visible propeller and yellow engine cowlings. The tail section has a conventional design with a single vertical stabilizer and horizontal stabilizers, and the fuselage displays additional insignia and markings. The cockpit is positioned at the front of the fuselage and appears to be enclosed with a multi-panel canopy. The airplane's surface is smooth and metallic, with visible panel lines and minimal surface texture.
description: A dark green, twin-propeller military airplane with low-mounted straight wings, white insignias, and a streamlined, enclosed cockpit at the front.
description_view_0: Front view showing the twin propellers, forward cockpit canopy, and broad wings with visible insignias.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the length of the fuselage, wing-mounted engines, and tail section with visible markings.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the tail assembly, horizontal and vertical stabilizers, and the full span of the wings.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing the other engine, cockpit placement, and additional insignia details.
attribute_wingspan_configuration: Low-wing configuration with straight wings and no visible winglets or pronounced dihedral/anhedral angles.
attribute_engine_type: Twin piston engines mounted on the wings, each with a visible propeller.
attribute_tail_design: Conventional tail with a single vertical stabilizer and two horizontal stabilizers.
attribute_landing_gear_type: Retractable taildragger landing gear with main gear under the wings and a tail wheel.
attribute_cockpit_placement: Forward-mounted, enclosed cockpit with a multi-panel canopy, likely accommodating multiple crew members.
